title: The Molecule of More
author: Daniel Z. Lieberman
subline: A single chemical drives love, sex, and creativity.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A stylized molecule. 8 carbon nodes (C), 11 hydrogens (H), 1 N, 2 O. The dopamine molecule's signature: a benzene ring on the right (6 carbons), and a side chain with N and 2 O. The OH on the ring is RED. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1700)" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5">
    <!-- Benzene ring (right) - 6 carbons in hexagon -->
    <g>
      <line x1="280" y1="-180" x2="280" y2="60"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="60" x2="450" y2="170"/>
      <line x1="450" y1="170" x2="620" y2="60"/>
      <line x1="620" y1="60" x2="620" y2="-180"/>
      <line x1="620" y1="-180" x2="450" y2="-290"/>
      <line x1="450" y1="-290" x2="280" y2="-180"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The interior hexagon (benzene inner ring) -->
    <g stroke-width="1" opacity="0.5">
      <line x1="280" y1="-180" x2="450" y2="-290"/>
      <line x1="450" y1="-290" x2="620" y2="-180"/>
      <line x1="620" y1="-180" x2="620" y2="60"/>
    </g>
    <!-- C atoms at vertices of benzene -->
    <g fill="#4c4c4c">
      <circle cx="280" cy="-180" r="8"/>
      <circle cx="450" cy="-290" r="8"/>
      <circle cx="620" cy="-180" r="8"/>
      <circle cx="620" cy="60" r="8"/>
      <circle cx="450" cy="170" r="8"/>
      <circle cx="280" cy="60" r="8"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The OH group (RED) -->
    <g stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2">
      <line x1="280" y1="-180" x2="160" y2="-280"/>
    </g>
    <circle cx="160" cy="-280" r="8" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <text x="160" y="-300" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.2em">OH</text>
    <!-- Side chain (CH2-CH2-NH2) - on the bottom-left of ring -->
    <g stroke-width="1.5">
      <line x1="280" y1="60" x2="120" y2="160"/>
      <line x1="120" y1="160" x2="-40" y2="60"/>
      <line x1="-40" y1="60" x2="-200" y2="160"/>
    </g>
    <g fill="#4c4c4c">
      <circle cx="120" cy="160" r="8"/>
      <circle cx="-40" cy="60" r="8"/>
    </g>
    <!-- N atom (nitrogen) at end -->
    <circle cx="-200" cy="160" r="10" fill="#4c4c4c"/>
    <text x="-200" y="200" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c">N</text>
    <text x="-200" y="218" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.1em">H₂</text>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="2200" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.4em">DOPAMINE MOLECULE · C₈H₁₁NO₂</text>
</svg>
rationale: The dopamine molecule diagram. A benzene ring on the right (6 carbon vertices), a side chain with N and 2 H atoms on the left, and the signature OH hydroxyl group at the top — the single element in RED. The molecule is a hairline graph in graphite, OH is 2px red stroke + red filled circle. Title 320px mixed case, weight 300, two lines. The composition is a chemistry textbook cover.
